H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble) is a HEALING Over‑stimulation DESCENT protocol with n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence strong. Qualitative data (n=29) reveal 66% positive change, typical 10‑minute settle, and high music appreciation. YouTube views=30, the highest in the dataset, but retention metrics are missing, so reach remains the primary signal. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, slightly below the observed mean, suggesting the protocol may be performing better than model expectations. Distribution is moderately right‑skewed with a concentration of scores 7–9.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners feel calmer within minutes, and the track is highly effective despite limited YouTube exposure.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (Rainfall) records n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence strong. Qualitative responses (n=21) show 65% positive change and mixed music sentiment, with some immediate settle reports. YouTube views=5 indicate low external exposure. AutoGluon does not provide a distinct prediction; the outcome spread is fairly tight. Distribution lacks pronounced bimodality.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Effectively reduces overstimulation, though a few listeners are more neutral about the music.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (Glacier) has n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence meaningful. Listeners (n=23) report notable improvements and settle times of 5–10 minutes. YouTube views=8, still limited. AutoGluon prediction is absent; the score distribution is symmetric around the mean.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Helps calm overstimulation and is generally well‑received.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Strongly helps with anxiety, delivering a quick sense of relief for most users.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Provides modest anxiety relief, though responses are a bit more varied.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Generally helpful for anxiety with a typical 10‑minute settling period.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In crisis moments the protocol offers noticeable stabilization for about half of the listeners.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Offers moderate calming during acute distress, with many reporting improvement.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Works for some in crisis, but music reactions vary, hinting at a need for adjustment.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (Lantern) is a HEALING Sleep & Rest DESCENT protocol with n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence strong. Qualitative data (n=15) indicate 65% positive change, rapid 5‑minute settle for many, and strong music liking. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for the sleep zone, closely matching the observed mean. Distribution shows a gentle right tail but no clear bimodality.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Helps listeners settle into rest quickly and is well liked.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (Hammock) records n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence meaningful. Qualitative feedback (n=15) includes mixed music opinions and some reports of ‘no effect,’ reflecting slightly broader response variance. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon does not supply a distinct prediction; the outcome distribution is fairly compact.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Provides similar sleep benefits, with a few neutral music opinions.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (Midnight) presents n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence meaningful. Listeners (n=22) report 64% positive change, settle within 5–10 minutes, and balanced rhythm impressions. YouTube views=3. No specific AutoGluon score; the distribution is unimodal around the mean.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Supports sleep and rest, though some find it less impactful.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (Compass) is a THRIVING Focus & Clarity HOLD protocol with n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence meaningful. Qualitative data (n=23) reveal 56% positive change, frequent ‘slightly busy’ rhythm comments, and settle times around 5 minutes. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for the focus zone, considerably above the observed mean, suggesting hidden efficacy that may be masked by rhythm perception. Distribution shows a broader spread.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus improvements are modest, and some find the rhythm a bit busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint) records n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence meaningful. Listeners (n=17) report 66% positive change and strong music liking. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for focus, aligning well with observed performance. Outcome distribution is relatively tight.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Focus enhancements are clearer and the music is well received.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (Summit) presents n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence meaningful. Qualitative feedback (n=29) shows 71% significant improvement, balanced rhythm perception, and typical 10‑minute settle. YouTube views=15, the highest within THRIVING. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, close to the observed mean, confirming strong efficacy. Distribution is broad, reflecting diverse listener experiences.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Delivers notable focus gains and is appreciated by many listeners.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (Ember) is a THRIVING Creative Flow HOLD protocol with n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence meaningful. Qualitative data (n=21) show modest improvements and rapid settling for many. YouTube views=2 indicate minimal reach. AutoGluon does not assign a distinct prediction; outcome spread is moderate.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Gives a gentle boost to creativity, though effects are modest.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (Current) records n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence meaningful. Listener comments (n=15) note higher improvement rates and favorable music. YouTube views=2. No AutoGluon prediction; distribution is tighter than L1.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Slightly stronger creative lift than L1, with consistent feedback.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (Aurora) has n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence developing. Qualitative responses (n=25) indicate 67% positive change and quick settle times. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon does not flag a distinct score; the distribution is slightly left‑skewed toward higher ratings.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Shows the strongest creative boost among its cohort.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (Stone) is a THRIVING Maintenance & Balance HOLD protocol with n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence meaningful. Qualitative responses (n=23) indicate moderate improvement and a long 20‑minute settle for many. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon does not provide a specific prediction; the outcome distribution is compact.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Supports balance and maintenance with steady, moderate benefits.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (Axis) records n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence meaningful, showing the highest variance among THRIVING protocols. Qualitative data (n=16) note mixed improvement levels and a split between 5‑ and 10‑minute settle times. YouTube views=4. No AutoGluon prediction; the wide spread may signal heterogeneous sub‑populations.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Results vary widely, indicating differing experiences of balance.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (Orbit) presents n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence developing. Listener comments (n=19) show 64% positive change, occasional ‘did NOT settle,’ and balanced rhythm impressions. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69 for the maintenance zone, slightly above the observed mean, hinting at potential under‑realized benefit. Distribution is moderately spread.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Shows promising balance benefits, though some users don’t feel settled.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (Breeze) is a TRANSFORMING Mood & Energy ASCENT protocol with n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence meaningful. Qualitative data (n=22) indicate 54% moderate improvement and strong music love. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts a high cross‑bucket score of 8.23, exceeding the observed mean, implying broader applicability. Distribution leans slightly right.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Boosts mood and energy for many, with strong positive feedback.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise) records n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence meaningful. Listeners (n=15) report 64% noticeable improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon does not assign a specific prediction; the outcome distribution is tight around the mean.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Similar mood benefits, with consistent listener appreciation.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (Updraft) presents n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence developing, with the lowest mean in the Mood & Energy series. Qualitative feedback (n=25) includes five ‘no effect’ reports, indicating possible incomplete entrainment. YouTube views=4. No AutoGluon prediction; the distribution shows a left‑skew toward lower scores.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mood impact is weaker, and several listeners report no effect.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (Footpath) is a TRANSFORMING Motivation & Drive ASCENT protocol with n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence meaningful. Qualitative comments (n=17) note 55% noticeable improvement and quick settle for many. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.88 for the motivation zone, substantially higher than observed, suggesting hidden potential. Distribution is wide with a possible low‑score tail.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation gains are modest, with potential for stronger impact.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (Trail) records n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence developing. Qualitative feedback (n=21) shows 43% noticeable improvement and mixed settle times. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon predicts 7.88 for motivation, again well above the observed mean, highlighting a discrepancy worth probing. Outcome spread is moderate.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Motivation effects are mixed, suggesting the need for more data.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (Switchback) presents n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence developing. Qualitative responses (n=17) reveal 62% positive change, quick settle, and mixed music sentiment. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon does not give a distinct prediction; the high variance suggests sensitivity to listener factors. Distribution appears bimodal with a high‑score cluster.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Shows promising motivation lifts for some, but experiences are varied.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (Cavern) is a TRANSFORMING Mind & Clarity DESCENT protocol with n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence meaningful. Qualitative responses (n=25) include 54% noticeable improvement and strong music love. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon does not list a direct prediction; outcome distribution is fairly symmetric.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Provides moderate mind‑clarity benefits, appreciated by many.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (Starlight) records n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence developing. Qualitative feedback (n=20) shows 56% significant improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views=12. AutoGluon predicts a zone score of 7.54, modestly higher than observed, suggesting possible under‑performance. Distribution is mildly right‑skewed.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Offers similar clarity with slightly higher perceived effectiveness.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos) presents n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence developing, with a markedly bimodal distribution and the lowest mean across all protocols. Qualitative data (n=17) indicate only 50% perceived benefit and mixed rhythm opinions. YouTube views=8, but low sample limits inference. AutoGluon predicts 7.54 for the mind‑clarity zone, a large gap signalling a strong candidate for redesign. Embedding drift is extreme (sim=0.443), reinforcing instability.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results are split; some users feel better, others notice little change.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across the three series the data shows consistent patterns that align with design intent while highlighting several outliers.
• HEALING yields the highest overall mean (≈7.0) with the largest sample and modest variance, suggesting stable efficacy.
• THRIVING matches HEALING in mean but displays tighter score dispersion and strong AutoGluon predictive signals.
• TRANSFORMING records the lowest mean (≈6.6) and the greatest variability, reflecting its ascending‑arc and low‑affect focus.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Protocol type and series shape distinct efficacy signatures.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols settle quickly (5–10 min) and maintain steady positive responses across contexts.
• THRIVING HOLD protocols achieve the highest predicted scores, especially in Focus & Clarity, with modest variance.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols show the broadest outcome spread and occasional under‑performance, consistent with their design for subtle state shifts.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A handful of protocols deviate notably from series norms, warranting focused scrutiny.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10) records a mean of 5.7, high std (2.45) and strong embedding drift (sim=0.443), indicating possible population mismatch.
• T-MB-L2 (n=22) exhibits elevated std (2.03) relative to its peers, suggesting heterogeneous listener responses.
• H-CR-L3 shows unexpected music‑opinion dissent and a slightly higher std (1.91) than other crisis protocols.
• X-MD-L2 displays a large AutoGluon prediction‑observed gap (pred 7.88 vs. mean 6.21), hinting at hidden efficacy signals.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Key actionable ideas emerge from convergent analyst observations.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 (n=10) – investigate subgroup split and prototype an L2 variant with adjusted Hz or shorter ascent.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-MB-L2 (n=22) – explore stratified routing or additional hold phase to manage response heterogeneity.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1 (n=95) – prioritize promotional effort to leverage strong efficacy with low YouTube reach.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 (n=35) – pilot cross‑bucket deployment into TRANSFORMING contexts, given AutoGluon cross‑bucket recommendation.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-AX-L1 (n=67) – adjust Adviser routing to favor anxiety‑stress listeners, aligning with high predicted efficacy.
• [SINGLE SOURCE] X-MD-L2 (n=14) – collect targeted data on motivation sub‑populations before redesign.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding diagnostics reveal both stability and evolution across protocols.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: fourteen protocols show notable drift (similarity < 0.85), especially within TRANSFORMING, indicating either genuine response shifts or analytical instability.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: H-OS-L2 ↔ H-OS-L3 (sim = 0.901) and X-ME-L1 ↔ X-MC-L1 (sim = 0.894) suggest functional redundancy; T-MB-L1 and T-MB-L3 are analytically isolated (mean_sim ≈ 0.56).
• Confidence note: drift metrics derive from limited historical runs; interpretations should be weighted accordingly.
